title: Ride Height / Level Sensor Calibration
description: Calibrates ride height sensors after sensor replacement or suspension component service. Affects headlight leveling, air suspension control, AFS (Adaptive Front Steering), and load-leveling systems.
common_symptoms: headlight leveling fault after suspension work
suspension warning light after sensor replacement
physical_solution: Park vehicle on flat level surface unloaded. Execute ride height sensor calibration routine. Vehicle learns reference height for all subsequent leveling calculations.

technical_solution.elm327_feasibility: Partial — BMW leveling sensor DID write via $22 read + $2E write accessible on F-series. VW/Audi ride height adaptation in CDC/air suspension module accessible pre-2019. Toyota HID leveling sensor $31 routine. Gateway restrictions on 2018+ Stellantis, 2019+ VW SFD.
preconditions: Vehicle on flat level surface (use alignment rack or confirmed flat floor)
Vehicle unloaded (driver only, no cargo, fuel tank 1/2 to full)
All suspension components fully installed and torqued
Vehicle at rest, engine ON (some OEMs require running for sensor check)

procedure_steps.description: Park vehicle on certified flat surface. No slopes, no ramps. Vehicle in normal ride height (not on jack stands). All doors closed. Driver seat occupied for some OEMs.
Enter air suspension or leveling control module extended session: $10 03. Module addresses: BMW VDC 0x60, VW/Audi air suspension 0x34, Toyota VDIM/leveling 0x7C0.
Read current ride height sensor values: $22 [HEIGHT_DID] for each corner (FL, FR, RL, RR). Values should be near reference spec. Note deviation from expected ride height.
Execute ride height calibration: $31 01 [HEIGHT_CALIB_ROUTINE]. For $2E path: $2E [HEIGHT_OFFSET_DID] [REFERENCE_VALUE] to write zero-point offset for each sensor.
After calibration: check headlight aiming. For HID/LED vehicles: aim projectors on flat wall 25ft away. Headlight leveling should automatically adjust with load changes.

Bounce each corner of vehicle (push down and release). Suspension should return to reference height. Load vehicle with weight (250 lbs in trunk): rear should level automatically on air suspension vehicles.

oem_notes.Toyota/Lexus: Land Cruiser/LX: KDSS + leveling sensors. HID auto-leveling sensor at front and rear. $31 calibration routine via Techstream or ELM327 extended session. Must be on flat surface, engine running.
oem_notes.Honda/Acura: Most models: fixed HID level sensor (no active leveling). MDX/Pilot: rear leveling sensor for load compensation. HDS path. ELM327: limited, most Honda leveling is passive.
oem_notes.Nissan/Infiniti: Infiniti Q70/QX80: air suspension leveling. CONSULT III+ for calibration. ELM327 $31 accessible on older platforms. Nissan Armada: auto-leveling rear suspension sensor calibration.
oem_notes.Ford: Ford Expedition/Navigator: air suspension leveling. IDS calibration. ELM327 accessible pre-2018. F-150: optional air suspension (Raptor/Tremor). Headlight leveling sensors on Fusion/Edge: $31 calibration.
oem_notes.GM: Cadillac Escalade/CT6, Chevy Suburban: active air suspension. GDS2 ride height calibration. Headlight leveling on most trucks: $2E write to leveling module. ELM327 accessible pre-2018.
oem_notes.BMW: Air suspension on 5/6/7 series and X5/X7. VDC (Vertical Dynamics Control) at 0x60. $22 DID reads per-corner height. $2E write for offset calibration. ISTA preferred for full calibration on G-series. F-series: ELM327 accessible.
oem_notes.VW/Audi: CDC (Continuous Damping Control) module at 0x34. Air suspension (AIRMATIC equiv): Audi allroad, Touareg, Q7. Ride height adaptation via ODIS/VCDS. $2E write accessible pre-2019 SFD. All 4 corner sensors must be calibrated separately.
oem_notes.Stellantis: Ram 1500 EAS (Electronic Air Suspension) leveling. wiTECH 2018+. Pre-2018: ELM327 accessible. Jeep Grand Cherokee: air suspension height calibration same path.
oem_notes.Hyundai/Kia: Genesis G90 / EQ900: air suspension with leveling. GDS2 calibration. ELM327 $31 accessible pre-2019. Most mainstream Hyundai/Kia: passive HID leveling only, no calibration needed.
